channel.title: Computer Science on Frank Huang
channel.description: Recent content in Computer Science on Frank Huang

item.title: How I build a dApps
item.description: 在之前的一篇关于blockchain的文章中，我大致记录了一下运作原理，虽然还有很多细节没有涉及，比如安全问题（51% computing power）等。这次我想主要记录一下一些以blockchain为主题的创业公司他们如何引入类似blockchain的decentralized的概念，也同时动手试着做一个dApps(decentralized apps)。由于写这篇博客的时间临近元旦，因此有比较充足的时间做调研。 Background 互联网时代已经从单台服务器(centralized)发展到了多台服务器为中心(distributed)，现在正在向去中心化(decentralized)发展。这些过程对应着web1.0， web2.0和web3.0。在decentralized的场景下，很重要的一个特性是单点的宕机并不会导致整个应用的失效，数据还有应用的代码都是分布在每个节点上的。因此 Research part 最开始一段时间主要是研究一下decentralized的 working dApps example: - steemit - IPFS sit down and think about the same time next year, where do you see yourself be specific praise yourself finish one web app for data presentation finish a kaggle competition independently get a travel movie done with gopro in order to achieve these goal, what should I do?

item.title: My understanding on blockchain so far
item.description: Block chain has been talked a lot in the field of high tech industry. Being able to understand this decentralized technology is a plus for my career and able to implement it practicely will even better. I will talk about some information I have learnt so far in this post including the implementation of blockchain using javascript. 继上次写的一篇博文What is bitcoin mining?之后，这次继续写下关于bitcoin技术的笔记。 相信看这篇文章的读者们应该或多或少对blockchain和bitcoin有所了解了，如果这里面涉及到的一些术语觉得不理解，可以试图从reference那里找到答案，实在不行可以wiki之或者直接评论告诉我哦。那我主要在这里梳理一下blockchain到底解决什么问题和其中我认为很有趣的技术细节。 bitcoin要解决的问题是去交易的中介，类似于银行在传统的交易模型中的地位，这是一个很直观而且亟待解决的问题。这样做似乎很理想，但是有两个很明显的问题需要解决： 1. 怎么确保每一笔先后发生的交易的先后顺序？ 2. 怎么确保在A到B的交易中，A有足够的金额发给B呢？

item.title: Different syntax for creating matrix and array in Different language
item.description: Golang grid := [][]int{ []int{1,2,3} []int{4,5,6} []int{7,8,9} } // declaring emptyGrid := [3][3]int C++ int a[3][4] = { {1,2,3}, {4,5,6}, {7,8,9} }; // or vector<vector<int>> grid(3, vector<int>(3)); Java ”‘java ”’

item.title: Differences for loop syntax in different language
item.description: Java for (int i; i < 100 ;i++){ // do something } Python for index, value in range(100){ # do something } Scala for (var a <- 1 to 10){ println(a) } <!-- or --> <!-- 10 not included --> for (var b <- 1 until 10){ println(b) } Go for i := 0; i < 100; i++ { fmt.Println(i) } Different useage of yield between python and scala def get_generator(): for i in range(10): if i % 3 ==0: yield i The yield statement should always inside a function to generate a generator object.

item.title: Learning Scala
item.description: I recently have to deal with scala to work with data ETL on a data warehouse. I found that scala is not like other language like java and python. Some of the expression is kind of new to me. I’m wondering why people use scala instead. Anyway, with this wonder, I decided to write a blog to record my learning process of SCALA along with the spark framework.

item.title: Learning concurrency programing with Java -- Snapshot 1.0
item.description: Reentrancy Each object has an intrinsic lock which when one thread is holding it, other thread could not request it. But reentrancy make the lock held by the thread itself be able to acquire it again. How does it work Each object holding a intrinsic lock will hold a count to the acquisition of that lock and the owning of the thread. Every reentrancy will cause the the count increment 1.

item.title: What is bitcoin mining?
item.description: Bitcoin has been in the field for a while and friends of mine have been talking about bitcoin for a while. I have also read some of the articles talking about bitcoin, the hashing and the trustful features. But to be honest, these concepts still remain a mist for me. I decided to really figure out them in the way that I think and try to explain it as clear as possible.

item.title: TCP connection and disconnection
item.description: TCP建立连接和断开链接一直是个让我很费解的问题，为什么要三番四次的来回折腾呢？于是我决定用形象的说法来讲一下我的理解。 为什么需要三次建立连接 如果采用两次握手的话，打个比方，去饭店吃饭，人太多了要拿号，拿号的动作(SYN_SEND)相当于第一次握手，店家给号的动作(SYN_RECV)相当于第二次握手，如果这样就结束了，那店家作为服务方就会跟你建立连接，也就是保证把位置给你，直到你要结束消费，但是很多情况是我们拿了号之后有可能觉得要等太久了，想换一家吃饭，结果就跑了，这样店家就会一直等我们，导致台位浪费，所以需要额外增加第三次的握手，也就是叫号的动作(ESTABLISHED)。 为什么分手还要四次啊 还是拿饭馆的例子说事，断开连接的过程实际上相当于我们入店消费完成以后要结账的过程。首先我们(client)跟服务员(server)招手，告诉他们我们需要结账(FIN_WAIT1)， 服务员看到后点头示意他们知道了(CLOSE_WAIT)，我们等服务员过来(FIN_WAIT2)，服务员过来以后，再次确认菜都上齐了并且告诉我们最后价格(LAST_ACK)，我们看了一下单子确认价格正确之后付款，然后我们进入(TIME_WAIT)，等待服务员确认收款，最后我们就可以愉快的走人啦。

item.title: Markdown Note
item.description: Building a website is full of fun and a sense of achievement. Markdown has been a popular technique for fullfilling the content of the website due to the efficiency and beauty. So I decided to learn about it and write down my very first blog to record the key point. Making Italic font? blablabla means blablabla Making bold font? blablabla means blablabla Making header? # header means first level header, ## header means second level header etc.

item.title: Pom configuration in Maven
item.description: package How to package all the dependencies into one jar <build> <plugins> <plugin> <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId> <artifactId>maven-assembly-plugin</artifactId> <version>2.5.5</version> <configuration> <archive> <manifest> <mainClass>com.xxg.Main</mainClass> </manifest> </archive> <descriptorRefs> <descriptorRef>jar-with-dependencies</descriptorRef> </descriptorRefs> </configuration> <executions> <execution> <id>make-assembly</id> <phase>package</phase> <goals> <goal>single</goal> </goals> </execution> </executions> </plugin> </plugins> </build> 不同环境下打包配置文件并在在工程中指定 package and single equals mvn pakcage assembly:single Reference
